original_text.name: Hebrew OT original + Strong's + morphology onboard
Greek NT original + Strong's + morphology onboard
read_passage tool: original + translation + lexical takes
lexicon takes wired (Strong's, then BDB / Thayer)
commentary takes searchable per verse (CCEL: Henry, Barnes, Calvin)
original_text.acquire: DONE 2026-06-15: cleaner source than CCEL found -- the Free Use Bible API (bible.helloao.org) serves classic public-domain commentaries as verse-keyed JSON. Built lw/00_source/commentary/matthew_henry.db (4,124 passage notes, all 66 books, 34MB) via tools/build_commentary_index.py (fetch+build, 1167 chapters). Wired commentary(reference, author) tool: passage-level, returns the note whose start verse covers the queried verse (John 3:16 -> Henry on the Nicodemus passage). ATTRIBUTED (Matthew Henry, public domain), never engine doctrine. More commentators (Barnes/Calvin/JFB/Clarke/Gill all on helloao) drop in by re-running the build with --commentary <id>.

original_text.name: sermon takes searchable (Spurgeon, Edwards, Wesley)
original_text.acquire: Public-domain sermons (Spurgeon's ~3500 NPSP/MTP, Edwards, Wesley) are prose, not verse-keyed. Source: CCEL + Spurgeon Center / archive.org plaintext. Plan: dedicated tick -- index by primary_scripture (each sermon already cites a text), build lw/00_source/sermons/*.db keyed verse->sermon, wire sermon_take(reference). Local data/sermons/sermons.jsonl holds only 3 of Matt's own outlines (not the public-domain corpus). ATTRIBUTED Layer-2.

original_text.name: BDB/Thayer richer lexical take layered onto read_passage
original_text.acquire: DONE 2026-06-15: STEPBible TBESH (Hebrew) + TBESG (Greek), CC BY -> lw/00_source/lexicon_bdbt/bdbt.db (19,570 entries: 8,723 H + 10,847 G). read_passage take.scholarly now carries a BDB/Thayer-grade definition per word (take-hit ~100%, filled the Strong's-lexicon gaps e.g. G25 agapao); standalone lexicon(strongs) tool added. Attributed Layer-0.

easy_wins: seal the crypto / info / game-theory spine
fix stale counts in the agent docs
bottlenecks: new verifiers are slow, exact, deterministic
engine finds, never generates -- bound by the discovered
best clinical / legal data is licence-gated
past the easy ceiling -- connection is nearly done
